name: Rico Magbanua
bio: Born and raised in the foothills of Mount Apo, I've been guiding treks and cultural tours across Davao Region for over 8 years. My passion is sharing the hidden gems of Mindanao with travelers from around the world.
storyExcerpt: Growing up in the foothills of Mount Apo, I learned to love the mountains before I could even read.
fullStory: Growing up in the foothills of Mount Apo, I learned to love the mountains before I could even read. My lolo would take me on trails every weekend, teaching me the names of every bird, tree, and river we passed. When I was 18, I started volunteering as a porter for trekking groups, and by 22, I had completed my DOT accreditation. Over 150 treks later, I still feel the same excitement every time I reach a summit with a new group of travelers. My mission is to share the beauty of Mindanao while protecting the land that raised me.

name: Amina Lidasan
bio: As a Maguindanaon guide, I specialize in cultural heritage tours across BARMM. I bridge visitors with the rich traditions, arts, and cuisine of Muslim Mindanao.
storyExcerpt: I want to change the narrative about Mindanao, one tour at a time.
fullStory: Growing up in Cotabato, I often heard negative stories about our homeland. I became a tour guide because I wanted to change the narrative — to show the world the beauty of our culture, the warmth of our people, and the incredible food that defines Maguindanaon hospitality. Every tour I lead is a chance to build bridges and create understanding.

name: Datu Bai Mendoza
bio: A T'boli community guide from Lake Sebu. I share our indigenous culture, from T'nalak weaving to traditional music, with visitors who want an authentic Mindanao experience.
storyExcerpt: As a T'boli descendant, I am honored to share our living culture with the world.
fullStory: My grandmother was a master T'nalak weaver. She taught me that our culture is not something kept in a museum — it is alive in every thread, every song, every sunset over Lake Sebu. When I became a community guide, I made it my mission to share this living heritage with visitors. Every tour starts with a prayer and ends with a story around the fire.

name: Marco Santos
bio: CDO's top adventure guide specializing in white water rafting, canyon climbing, and zipline experiences. Safety-certified with 10+ years of experience.
storyExcerpt: I've been chasing rapids and adrenaline rushes across Northern Mindanao for a decade.
fullStory: CDO is the adventure capital of the Philippines, and I've spent the last decade proving it. From Class IV rapids on the Cagayan River to the heights of the Sky Challenge zipline, I've guided thousands of thrill-seekers safely through Northern Mindanao's most exciting experiences. Safety is always first, but fun is a very close second.

name: Jasmine Tan
bio: GenSan's food tourism specialist! I take visitors through the Tuna Capital's best markets, seafood restaurants, and hidden local eateries.
storyExcerpt: They call GenSan the Tuna Capital — I call it the Food Capital of Mindanao.
fullStory: Growing up in a family of fisherfolk in General Santos, food was always at the center of our lives. I started hosting food tours after visitors kept asking me where to find the best tuna sashimi. Now I guide visitors through markets, kitchens, and secret local spots that you won't find in any guidebook.

name: Khalil Omar
bio: A Maranao guide dedicated to rebuilding tourism in Marawi through heritage tours of Islamic architecture, Maranao crafts, and lakeside experiences.
storyExcerpt: Tourism is peace-building. Every visitor to Marawi is a step toward healing.
fullStory: After the siege, many said Marawi was finished as a tourism destination. I disagree. Our mosques, our lake, our crafts — they survived, and so did our spirit. I guide visitors through Marawi not just to show them beautiful places, but to show them that peace is possible, and that tourism can be a tool for healing and understanding.
